stem: Most modern CPUs are based on the von Neumann architecture.
model_answer: In a von Neumann architecture both program instructions and data are stored in the same main memory and are accessed via the same bus, one at a time. A single CPU with a control unit and ALU fetches, decodes and executes these instructions sequentially.

parts.label: (a)
parts.prompt: Describe two features of the von Neumann architecture.

mark_points.text: instructions and data are stored together in the same main memory
accepted_alternatives: data and program share one memory

mark_points.text: a single processor fetches, decodes and executes instructions sequentially using one bus at a time
accepted_alternatives: instructions are processed one at a time
uses a single bus between CPU and memory
